entries.title: as evidence that LLM-mediated text-to-geometry is viable for complex tasks.
The Reflective Practitioner: How Professionals Think in Action
entries.abstract: Foundational text on reflective practice in professional work. The CLI's OK:/ERROR: protocol operationalizes Schon's reflective conversation with the situation.

entries.title: Understanding the Blind: An Integrative Approach to Visual Impairment
entries.abstract: Comprehensive account of blind spatial cognition, sequential landmark-based reasoning, and language-mediated spatial understanding.

entries.title: Spatial representations and navigation in blind people
entries.abstract: Review of blind spatial cognition research including mental maps, wayfinding strategies, and non-visual spatial representation.

entries.title: Spatial learning and navigation in blindness
entries.abstract: Updated framework for blind spatial cognition. Key reference for the project's semantic-over-geometric approach.

entries.title: Architecture's New Media: Principles, Theories, and Methods of Computer-Aided Design
entries.abstract: Process as first-class artifact alongside product. Relevant to version-controlled, auditable design workflows.

entries.title: Digital Design and Manufacturing: CAD/CAM Applications in Architecture and Design
entries.abstract: ACADIA 2024 Teaching Award work. Fabrication-centered pedagogy, physical making as cognitive partner.

entries.title: Theory of Design in the Age of Computation
entries.abstract: Reflective practice over routine production. Framework for understanding computational design pedagogy.

entries.title: The Space Between: Architecture and Disability
entries.abstract: Beyond access: disability as architectural knowledge. Challenges accommodation-as-minimum-compliance.

entries.title: Guidelines and Standards for Tactile Graphics
entries.abstract: BANA guidelines for tactile graphic production. Standards for braille labels, line weights, density ranges used in PIAF output.

entries.title: Universal Design in Higher Education: From Principles to Practice
entries.abstract: Framework for universal design in educational settings. Background for inclusive studio pedagogy.

entries.title: Disability, Architecture, and Infrastructure
entries.abstract: Building Access: Universal Design and the Politics of Disability. Critical disability studies framework for architectural accessibility.

entries.title: Towards a Critical Technical Practice: Accessible Design in Architecture
entries.abstract: Diversity and Design: Understanding Hidden Consequences. Critical approach to inclusive design practice.

entries.title: Tactile maps for the blind: A case study in multisensory design
entries.abstract: Research on tactile map design for blind users. Methods for translating visual spatial information to tactile form.

entries.title: Haptic perception of spatial relations by blind and sighted participants
entries.abstract: Touch, Blindness, and Neuroscience. Research on haptic spatial reasoning relevant to tactile architectural models.

entries.title: Design for Architecture in the Digital Era
entries.abstract: Architecture in the Digital Age: Design and Manufacturing. Foundational text on computational design workflows.

entries.title: Model Context Protocol: Connecting AI to tools
entries.abstract: MCP protocol specification for connecting AI assistants to external tools and data sources. Used for the Layout Jig MCP server.

entries.title: Exploring Expressive Tactile Design: Tactile Graphics Beyond Utility
entries.abstract: Research on tactile graphics as expressive medium beyond purely functional representation.

entries.title: Non-Visual Access to Computing
entries.abstract: Overview of non-visual computing interfaces including screen readers, tactile displays, and auditory interfaces.

entries.title: How can basic research on spatial cognition enhance the visual accessibility of architecture for people with low vision?
entries.abstract: Defines functional challenges in perception and spatial cognition with restricted visual information. Reviews empirical work on low vision perception of spaces and evaluates how results provide insights for architects designing visually accessible spaces.

entries.title: Architects and Visually Impaired People: Teaching Architecture with and for People Born Blind
entries.abstract: Advances disability as a resource to complement designers' visual way of knowing. Shows architecture students are unaware of how common building materials feel and unable to identify them by touch.

entries.title: Making Engineering Education Accessible to Blind and Low-Vision Learners
entries.abstract: Details how the first blind student to enroll in an introductory electrical engineering course at Stanford navigated the class, identifying accessibility gaps and solutions. Won Best DEI Paper at the 2024 ASEE conference.

entries.title: Cognitive map formation in the blind is enhanced by three-dimensional tactile information
entries.abstract: Compared equivalent 2D swell paper and 3D printed tactile maps. Early blind participants showed stronger cognitive map formation with 3D mazes. 3D maps produced with Prusa i3 MK2S printer; 2D maps with PIAF Tactile Image Maker.

entries.title: Cognitive map formation through tactile map navigation in visually impaired and sighted persons
entries.abstract: Investigates cognitive map formation via tactile map navigation, finding that after extensive training, persons with visual impairments and sighted persons form equally accurate mental representations of tactile map layouts.

entries.title: A Systematic Literature Review on the Automatic Creation of Tactile Graphics for the Blind and Visually Impaired
entries.abstract: Systematic review screening 250+ papers on automatic tactile graphic generation. Reviews thermoforming, swell paper, embossing, and special printers (PIAF, ViewPlus Tiger, Index Braille).

entries.title: Automatic (Tactile) Map Generation -- A Systematic Literature Review
entries.abstract: Reviews algorithms and models for map generalization and automatic tactile map generation. Screened 500+ studies; found no comprehensive automatic solution yet exists.

entries.title: Using Tactile Maps to Improve the Practical Spatial Knowledge of Adults who are Blind
entries.abstract: Evaluated effects of direct experience, cartographic representation, and verbal description on spatial knowledge of 30 blind adults. Found practical spatial knowledge was better when participants learned routes with a tactile map.

entries.title: Spatial navigation by congenitally blind individuals
entries.abstract: Reviews advances in neuroscience regarding spatial navigation by blind and sighted people. Addresses lack of consensus on the extent to which locomotion and wayfinding depend on amodal spatial representations.

entries.title: Wayfinding in the blind: larger hippocampal volume and supranormal spatial navigation
entries.abstract: Shows blind individuals possess superior navigational skills on route learning tasks compared to sighted controls, and demonstrates significant volume increase of the hippocampus in blind individuals.

entries.title: AI, architecture, accessibility, and data justice -- ACADIA special issue
entries.abstract: Explores the current status of AI and ML learning frameworks in design and probes new methods of creation relative to data access and data justice.

entries.title: Bridging Pixels and Fabrication: Enhancing Accessibility in CNC Knitting for Architectural Design
entries.abstract: Explores using Kniterate CNC knitting machine with Grasshopper plug-in to enhance accessibility for designers and architects. Won the Vanguard Award at ACADIA 2024.

entries.title: From Cluttered to Clear: Improving the Web Accessibility Design for Screen Reader Users in E-commerce With Generative AI
entries.abstract: Presents a GenAI-based browser extension for automated optimization of website structure and content for screen reader users.

entries.title: Information Wayfinding of Screen Reader Users: Five Personas to Expand Conceptualizations of User Experiences
entries.abstract: Distills notable patterns of screen reader use and presents five archetypal personas of screen reader users to address the gap in disability personas for design.

entries.title: Rich Screen Reader Experiences for Accessible Data Visualization
entries.abstract: Identifies three design dimensions (structure, navigation, description) for screen-reader-accessible visualizations. Best Paper Honorable Mention at EuroVis.

entries.title: Chart Reader: Accessible Visualization Experiences Designed with Screen Reader Users
entries.abstract: Co-design study with 10 BLV screen reader users to develop accessible visualization experiences. Synthesizes hierarchical navigation, sonification, and cross-cutting insights.

entries.title: Understanding Screen-Reader Users' Experiences with Online Data Visualizations
entries.abstract: Found that BLV users spend 211% more time interacting with charts and are 61% less accurate in extracting information. 33% of visualizations were undiscoverable to screen readers.

entries.title: The Impact of AI on Developer Productivity: Evidence from GitHub Copilot
entries.abstract: Controlled experiment where treatment group with AI pair programmer completed HTTP server implementation 55.8% faster. Developers with less experience benefited most.

entries.title: Who is Using AI to Code? Global Diffusion and Impact of Generative AI
entries.abstract: Applied neural classifier to 30M+ commits from 160,097 developers across six countries. By end of 2024, ~29% of Python functions had substantial AI support.

entries.title: Inclusive Design Studios: Rethinking the Instructor's Role
entries.abstract: Drawing on research on expertise cultivation, student motivation, and stereotype threat, considers how to rethink design studio instruction to be more inclusive.
entries.venue: Enquiry: The ARCC Journal for Architectural Research

entries.title: The emerging intersection: the role of architecture in promoting inclusive education
entries.abstract: Critically reviews literature published 2010-2022 on architecture's role in inclusive education. Argues universal design principles need to be applied for accessible architectural designs.

entries.title: Universal Design for Learning and Instruction: Effective Strategies for Inclusive Higher Education
entries.abstract: Universal Design originated in architecture by Ron Mace to promote accessible design. Explores how UDL and UDI emerge as teaching strategies encouraging inclusion in education.

entries.title: Haptic design research: A blind sense of space
entries.abstract: Develops haptic design parameters for architects based on classification of haptics into active, dynamic and passive touch. Parameters derived from expertise of people born blind.
entries.venue: International Association of Societies of Design Research (IASDR)

entries.title: Designing Architecture for More: A Framework of Haptic Design Parameters with the Experience of People Born Blind
entries.abstract: Framework to support architects in paying attention to role of touch during design. Characteristics based on expertise of people born blind and professional caregivers.

entries.title: Towards Automatically Generated Tactile Detail Maps by 3D Printers for Blind Persons
entries.abstract: Introduces approach for semi-automatic generation of worldwide available detailed tactile maps including buildings and blind-specific features. Generates 2.1D tactile models printable by consumer 3D printers.

entries.title: Interactive 3D Printed Urban Maps for Blind People
entries.abstract: Proposes haptic device allowing blind users to explore object locations on a 3D physical model with audio descriptions activated by tapping. Study with 11 blind participants received positive feedback.

entries.title: Map Learning with a 3D Printed Interactive Small-Scale Model: Improvement of Space and Text Memorization in Visually Impaired Students
entries.abstract: Investigates 3D printed interactive small-scale models for map learning by visually impaired students. Addresses problem of working memory overload from back-and-forth between tactile drawing and braille legend.

entries.title: shapeCAD: An Accessible 3D Modelling Workflow for the Blind and Visually-Impaired Via 2.5D Shape Displays
entries.abstract: Co-designed with three blind users to create accessible 3D modelling using script-based CAD (OpenSCAD) and a 2.5D tactile pin shape display. Users can haptically explore, modify, and author new 3D models.

entries.title: A Practical CAD Method for the Visually Impaired: A Case of Modeling the Leaning Tower of Pisa
entries.abstract: Author, who is totally blind, devised a form of programmable CAD modeling not requiring screen confirmation. Subjective evaluation confirmed models comparable to those by sighted users.

entries.title: Enabling Uniform Computer Interaction Experience for Blind Users through Large Language Models
entries.abstract: Developed Savant, an LLM-powered assistive technology that allows blind screen reader users to interact uniformly with any application interface through natural language.

entries.title: I look at it as the king of knowledge: How Blind People Use and Understand Generative AI Tools
entries.abstract: Rich empirical study of how blind people integrate GenAI tools to enhance productivity and access information in their regular work practices.

entries.title: TactileNet: Bridging the Accessibility Gap with AI-Generated Tactile Graphics for Individuals with Vision Impairment
entries.abstract: AI-based approach that automates tactile graphic generation to accelerate production and democratize access to educational materials for visually impaired learners.

entries.title: Universal design, inclusive design, accessible design, design for all: different concepts -- one goal?
entries.abstract: Discusses UD as an interdisciplinary topic relevant for rehabilitation and building professions. Argues the UN CRPD pre-supposes a relational model of disability.
entries.venue: Universal Access in the Information Society

entries.title: Universal Design Research as a New Materialist Practice
entries.abstract: Returns to Universal Design as a design phenomenon, arguing that critical disability theory should understand UD research to formulate a nuanced, new materialist disability epistemology.

entries.title: Guidelines for Standardizing the Design of Tactile Maps: A Review of Research and Best Practice
entries.abstract: Reviews research and best practices for standardizing the design of tactile maps for blind and visually impaired users.

entries.title: Chart4Blind: An Intelligent Interface for Chart Accessibility Conversion
entries.abstract: Intelligent user interface that converts bitmap image representations of line charts into universally accessible formats for BVI individuals.

entries.title: Advancing Accessible 3D Design for the Blind and Visually-Impaired via Tactile Shape Displays
entries.abstract: Explores tactile shape displays for making 3D design accessible to blind and visually-impaired users.

entries.title: Enhancing universal design through co-design: perceptions of practice, process and outcomes
entries.abstract: Examines how co-design is implemented in the design of large-scale public buildings with people with disability. Argues for embedding co-design and universal design as intertwined, equity-driven practices.

entries.title: Experimental study about 3D printed tactile symbols for tactile maps and blind users
entries.abstract: Experimental evaluation of 3D printed tactile symbols for use in tactile maps by blind users.
entries.venue: Journal of Accessibility and Design for All (JACCES)
